title: Make a financial declaration
description: Public officials and other named people must declare their assets, liabilities and income every year.
summary: Some public officials and senior officers must make a financial declaration each year. The declaration sets out their assets, their liabilities, their income, and the same details for their spouse and any minor children.

The duty to declare comes from the Prevention of Corruption Act and from other laws covering specific public roles. If you have been asked to declare, you will normally have received a written notice telling you what to include and when it is due.
The form is long. It covers property, vehicles, bank and investment accounts, loans and mortgages, income, and gifts of $500 BBD or more received during the year.
Late or incomplete declarations can lead to investigation. If you cannot get all the information in time, tell the Commission in writing so they know you are still working on it.
who_can_apply: people in public office named under the Prevention of Corruption Act
directors and senior officers of state-owned enterprises
other people the law says must declare

items: your full name, address and the parish you live in
details of every property you own, in Barbados and overseas
the value and account numbers of your bank, credit union and investment accounts
details of your vehicles and other valuable property
every loan, mortgage and debt you have
the same details for your spouse and any minor children
any gifts of $500 BBD or more you received in the year

items.name: Bank, credit union and investment statements

how_long: Submit your declaration by the date in the notice you received.

options.heading: In person
content: Take your sworn declaration to the Integrity Commission or other office named in the law you are declaring under.

edge_cases.heading: If you cannot get all the details in time
content: Tell the Commission in writing. Late or incomplete declarations may be referred for investigation.

contact.agency: Corporate Affairs and Intellectual Property Office (CAIPO)
contact.address:
Baobab Tower, Ground Floor
Warrens
St. Michael

assumptions: ‘Some public officials, directors and senior officers’ – the exact list of people who must declare depends on which law you fall under. The form is generic.
‘Gifts of $500 BBD or more’ is the threshold typically used. The form may use a different figure.
‘Submit by the date in the notice you received’ – the form itself does not state a universal deadline.
